epicTitle: Pluggable Blueprint Engine with AutoGuide Adapter + VisuDEV Enrichment

bodySections.intent: Define a clean, narrow blueprint-provider contract so legacy and AutoGuide providers can be registered and selected interchangeably.
bodySections.userJourney: Engineer opens Local Engine config and sees a typed registry of blueprint providers (`legacy-blueprint-runner`, `autoguide`). Adding a future provider requires only implementing the interface and registering it.
bodySections.problem: Current `AnalysisProvider` is too broad (covers appflow/data too) and provider selection is buried in a hard-coded `resolveBlueprintProviderId` function. There is no intermediate raw-scan contract.
bodySections.solution: Introduce `BlueprintProvider` interface with `scan(input) → Promise<RawBlueprintScan>`. Keep `AnalysisProvider` for non-blueprint scans. Update `AnalysisService` registry to distinguish blueprint providers from other providers.
bodySections.edgeCases: A provider ID that is not registered must return a clear `PROVIDER_NOT_FOUND` error. Unknown future provider IDs must not break existing appflow/data runs.
acceptance: `BlueprintProvider` interface exists in `local-engine/src/providers/blueprint-provider.interface.ts`.
`AnalysisService` stores blueprint providers separately from appflow/data providers.
`providerIdForScanType` still maps `blueprint` to the selected blueprint provider.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: yes; github: yes (shared types only).

bodySections.intent: Eliminate the `parent.childRuns is not iterable` error by validating whether a status file actually represents a parent run before iterating children.
bodySections.userJourney: User creates a new project and clicks “Neu analysieren” in Blueprint view. The status endpoint returns a clear JSON status instead of throwing TypeError.
bodySections.problem: `readParentRun` casts the JSON file to `EngineParentAnalysisRun | null` without runtime validation. If the file is a leaf-run status (or corrupted), `syncParentChildStatuses` iterates `undefined`.
bodySections.solution: Add `isParentAnalysisRun(value)` type guard checking `scanType === 'all'` and `Array.isArray(childRuns)`. Guard `syncParentChildStatuses` and `getStatus`. Return `RUN_STATUS_INVALID` if a leaf run is misread as parent.
bodySections.edgeCases: Partially written status, missing `childRuns`, and leaf-run IDs polled as parent must all fail gracefully with structured errors.
acceptance: `parent.childRuns is not iterable` no longer appears in logs or UI.
`getStatus` returns `{ success: false, error: { code: 'RUN_STATUS_INVALID' } }` when status file is not a valid parent run.
Unit tests cover leaf-run misread and missing `childRuns` scenarios.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: yes; github: yes (same shared types).

bodySections.intent: Allow each VisuDEV project to choose its blueprint provider independently, falling back to the global env default.
bodySections.userJourney: In project creation/editing, user selects “Blueprint Provider” (AutoGuide / Legacy). The selected provider is stored with the project and used for subsequent blueprint scans.
bodySections.problem: `VISUDEV_ANALYSIS_PROVIDER` is global; a user cannot compare AutoGuide and legacy output on two different projects side-by-side.
bodySections.solution: Add `blueprintProviderId` to `LocalVisuDevProject` and project DTO. Persist in `projects.json`. Expose on `POST /api/projects` and `PATCH /api/projects/:id`. `AnalysisService` resolves provider per project, falling back to env default.
bodySections.edgeCases: Invalid/missing provider ID falls back to env default, then `legacy-blueprint-runner`. Changing provider after a scan does not corrupt previous blueprint cache.
acceptance: `LocalVisuDevProject` has optional `blueprintProviderId`.
Create and update project APIs accept and persist `blueprintProviderId`.
`AnalysisService.startAnalysis` resolves provider from project first, then config.
`GET /api/capabilities` still lists available providers.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: yes; github: yes (projects.json / Supabase project row).

bodySections.intent: Decouple raw scan output from the final `BlueprintDocument` so VisuDEV enrichment is independent of the analysis provider.
bodySections.userJourney: Whether the scan came from AutoGuide or legacy runner, the Blueprint page receives the same canonical shape (routes, securityMatrix, findings, facts).
bodySections.problem: AutoGuide mapper currently builds the entire `BlueprintDocument` inline, duplicating defaults and making it hard to enrich consistently across providers.
bodySections.solution: Create `RawBlueprintScan` DTO (routes, facts, filesAnalyzed, providerMetadata). Build `BlueprintEnrichmentService.enrich(input)` that returns a typed `BlueprintDocument` with normalized routes, default project profile, and extension points for Security Matrix / findings.
bodySections.edgeCases: Empty raw scans still produce a valid empty `BlueprintDocument` (routes=[], findings=[], etc.). Unknown provider metadata is preserved under a namespaced key.
acceptance: `RawBlueprintScan` type exists in shared API types.
`BlueprintEnrichmentService` exists in `local-engine/src/services/blueprint-enrichment.service.ts`.
Both providers can be routed through the same enrichment step.
Output passes `normalizeBlueprintData` on the client without data loss.
`npm run checks` green.

issues.title: Refactor legacy blueprint provider to raw DTO

bodySections.intent: Make the legacy Preview-Runner blueprint provider return a `RawBlueprintScan` instead of a fully-formed `BlueprintDocument`.
bodySections.userJourney: Legacy scan completes and enrichment service normalizes its output exactly like AutoGuide output, so UI behavior is consistent.
bodySections.problem: Legacy provider currently builds `BlueprintDocument` directly; route shapes and security matrix may differ from AutoGuide output.
bodySections.solution: Update `LegacyVisuDevAnalysisProvider` to parse the runner response into `RawBlueprintScan` (routes, facts, raw metadata) and return `kind: 'blueprintRaw'`. `AnalysisService` passes the raw result to `BlueprintEnrichmentService`.
bodySections.edgeCases: Legacy runner still returns full legacy shape on its HTTP API; only the Local Engine provider mapping changes. Missing or partial runner response handled gracefully.
acceptance: Legacy provider returns raw scan shape consumed by enrichment service.
Final persisted `blueprint.json` remains compatible with `BlueprintPage`.
Regression: legacy provider still default when no override is set.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: yes; github: n/a (legacy runner is local-only).

issues.title: Refactor AutoGuide adapter to raw DTO

bodySections.intent: Make AutoGuide provider return the same `RawBlueprintScan` shape so it can share VisuDEV enrichment with the legacy provider.
bodySections.userJourney: With `VISUDEV_ANALYSIS_PROVIDER=autoguide`, the scan produces the same canonical blueprint page as legacy, enriched by VisuDEV.
bodySections.problem: `autoguide-to-blueprint.mapper.ts` builds a complete `BlueprintDocument` with hard-coded defaults. It duplicates enrichment logic that should be shared.
bodySections.solution: Replace mapper with `autoguide-to-raw.mapper.ts` producing `RawBlueprintScan`. Preserve original AutoGuide metadata under `providerMetadata.autoguide`. Route and fact IDs become stable. Enrichment service consumes the raw scan.
bodySections.edgeCases: `@autoguide/scanner` unavailable still returns `AUTOGUIDE_PACKAGES_UNAVAILABLE`. Empty routes/elements produce empty but valid raw scan.
acceptance: AutoGuide provider returns raw scan shape.
Old `autoguide-to-blueprint.mapper.ts` logic is moved or replaced; no duplication with enrichment service.
Final blueprint still renders Security Matrix, Route Canvas, and Findings.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: yes; github: n/a (AutoGuide load is local-only).

issues.title: Security Matrix + Findings enrichment
dependsOn: Refactor legacy blueprint provider to raw DTO
Refactor AutoGuide adapter to raw DTO

bodySections.intent: Populate Security Matrix and VisuDEV-specific findings from the canonical raw scan, independent of provider.
bodySections.userJourney: After any blueprint scan, the Security Matrix shows one row per route with auth/validation/role states, and the Finding Inspector lists maintainability/security findings derived from facts.
bodySections.problem: Security Matrix is currently scaffolded with all `unknown/n/a` in AutoGuide mapper and may differ in legacy output. Findings are limited to aria-label hints.
bodySections.solution: Extend `BlueprintEnrichmentService` with `buildSecurityMatrix(routes, facts)` and `buildFindings(routes, facts)`. Matrix columns: auth, role, validation, rateLimit, db, rls, audit. Initial state derived from heuristics (e.g., presence of auth middleware in file path, validation imports, RLS policy strings). Findings: missing aria-label, unvalidated route parameters, missing auth guard on mutating routes.
bodySections.edgeCases: Routes with no evidence keep `unknown` state, not false positives. Mutating methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) flagged more strictly for auth than GET.
acceptance: Security Matrix rows align with normalized routes for both providers.
At least three finding rules run heuristically on raw facts.
`BlueprintPage` displays matrix and findings without provider-specific branching.
`npm run checks` green.

bodySections.intent: Let users choose a project’s blueprint provider without editing env files.
bodySections.userJourney: On the Projects page, the create/edit form includes a “Blueprint-Anbieter” select with “Systemstandard / Legacy / AutoGuide”.
bodySections.problem: Provider selection is hidden behind environment variables, making it hard to experiment.
bodySections.solution: Add a DaisyUI select in `ProjectsPage` create/edit form. Options map to `undefined | 'legacy-blueprint-runner' | 'autoguide'`. Disabled when `analysis.autoguide.available === false`. Persist via existing project API.
bodySections.edgeCases: Supabase mode hides the selector or shows read-only fallback. Invalid provider ID from API falls back gracefully.
acceptance: Select appears in project creation and editing form.
AutoGuide option disabled when packages unavailable.
Selected value persisted and used on next blueprint scan.
`npm run checks` green.
bodySections.runtime: local: primary; github: selector hidden or read-only.

bodySections.intent: Document the new pluggable provider model so team members know how to switch providers and add new ones.
bodySections.userJourney: Developer reads `docs/LOCAL_ENGINE.md` and understands: default provider, per-project override, how to add a new `BlueprintProvider`, and how enrichment works.
bodySections.problem: Docs currently only describe env-based AutoGuide enablement, not the broader provider architecture.
bodySections.solution: Update `docs/LOCAL_ENGINE.md` with a “Pluggable Blueprint Providers” section. Add optional `VISUDEV_DEFAULT_BLUEPRINT_PROVIDER` to `.env.local.example` if present. Mention new `BlueprintProvider` interface.
bodySections.edgeCases: If `.env.local.example` does not exist, skip or create only if consistent with project conventions.
acceptance: `docs/LOCAL_ENGINE.md` explains provider interface, registry, per-project selection, and enrichment.
Env examples mention default provider configuration.
No dead references to removed files.
`npm run checks` green.

issues.title: Regression tests + npm run checks green

bodySections.intent: Ensure the refactored provider and enrichment pipeline does not break existing blueprint behavior.
bodySections.userJourney: Running `npm run checks` after all changes passes lint, typecheck, tests, and build.
bodySections.problem: Wide refactor across providers, service, and shared types risks type or runtime regressions.
bodySections.solution: Add/update unit tests for: provider registry, status handling, enrichment service, legacy raw DTO mapping, AutoGuide raw DTO mapping. Run `npm run checks` and fix all warnings/errors.
bodySections.edgeCases: Tests must run without real AutoGuide packages installed (mock raw scan). Legacy runner tests mock HTTP response.
acceptance: Unit tests cover provider registry, status handling, and enrichment.
`npm run checks` exits 0 with zero warnings.
Manual smoke test: create project → blueprint scan via legacy → no `childRuns` error.
